sourceRepository.preservationRole: source history, citation context, and audit provenance

artifactFamilies.description: Multi-tier annotated corpus of Lincoln rhetoric (1838-1865): 48-doc core, 75-100 doc validation, and search-only reference sets
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Lincoln's own writings are public domain. Third-party commentary, translations, and editorial apparatus require rights review before import.

artifactFamilies.description: Stage-by-stage annotation artifacts: segmentation (Stage 4A model scoring), clustering (Stage 4A outputs), human coding (Stage 4H), adjudication (Stage 4J)
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Annotation artifacts are original work of this project; no third-party rights apply.

artifactFamilies.description: Inter-annotator reliability studies across v2 (multi-model), v3 (human IAA), and planned v4 sampling designs
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Reliability records are original work of this project.

artifactFamilies.description: Codebook, stage definitions, annotation guidelines, and corpus expansion architecture documentation
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Original work; may be referenced from PPW method notes.

artifactFamilies.description: Quarto publication site with per-stage result pages, methodology documentation, and release checklists
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Child-repo site remains live as primary publication. PPW site will reference it.

artifactFamilies.description: Python and shell pipeline scripts for corpus ingestion, segmentation, annotation, and validation
artifactFamilies.rightsNotes: Child-repo-specific; inform PPW pipeline design but are not imported.

supersessionNote: Child-repo issue tracking superseded by PPW Milestone 12 migration. New implementation work is coordinated through PPW. Source repository is preserved as history and audit context.
